differential_diagnoses: Thyrotoxicosis (e.g., painless thyroiditis or recurrence of Graves' disease)
Delirium secondary to metabolic or endocrine abnormality
next_steps: Obtain emergent thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4, total T3, TPO antibodies, TSH-receptor antibodies)
Send CSF for autoimmune encephalitis panel (including NMDA receptor antibodies)
Monitor for hemodynamic instability and signs of thyroid storm (Burch-Wartofsky Point Scale)
Consider formal neuropsychological testing once stable

differential_diagnoses: Thyrotoxic crisis (thyroid storm) secondary to Graves' disease
Delirium secondary to metabolic or endocrine dysfunction
next_steps: Obtain emergent thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4, total T3)
Calculate Burch-Wartofsky Point Scale score and initiate supportive treatment for potential thyroid storm
Test serum for anti-thyroid peroxidase (TPO) antibodies and TSH-receptor antibodies
Continuous cardiac telemetry and hemodynamic monitoring
Review collateral history for possible access to exogenous thyroid hormones
Consult Endocrinology for urgent management of thyrotoxicosis
Send CSF for autoimmune encephalitis panel and paraneoplastic evaluation
Consult Neurology and Psychiatry for multidisciplinary management
